response.justification: While Bogdan is an accomplished full-stack engineer with 18+ years of experience and strong software craftsmanship practices, he fundamentally misaligns with this C++ systems programming role. His C++ experience is minimal (6 months in 2014–2015) and outdated; his current focus is TypeScript/Node.js backend development. More critically, he has zero background in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or the security standards (IEC 62443, SDL) that are central to the role. Strong general engineering skills cannot offset the domain and technical expertise gaps required for a mid-senior C++ position in industrial machinery manufacturing.
strengths: Extensive software development experience (18+ years) exceeds the 8–12 year requirement, with demonstrated ability to architect complex systems and guide code quality practices
Strong emphasis on testing, code craftsmanship, and architectural thinking, evidenced by open-source projects (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and mentoring through workshops and Code Kata sessions
Proven experience building high-performance, event-driven systems at Dunnhumby (low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/second) showing systems-level thinking
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap: only 6 months of C++ work in 2014–2015; current stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and D, with no recent systems-level programming experience
No industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or machinery background; unfamiliar with industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA) or manufacturing domain contexts
Missing industrial cybersecurity and standards knowledge (no IEC 62443, SDL implementation, or secure development lifecycle experience mentioned)
